rubric_name: IELTS Academic Writing Task 2 – public band descriptors

dimensions.name: task_response
dimensions.description: How fully the task is addressed; position; relevance, extension and support of ideas.

bands.9: Fully addresses all parts of the task and presents a fully developed position in answer to the question with relevant, fully extended and well supported ideas.
bands.8: Sufficiently addresses all parts of the task and presents a well developed response to the question with relevant, extended and supported ideas.
bands.7: Addresses all parts of the task; presents a clear position throughout; presents, extends and supports main ideas, but there may be a tendency to over-generalise and/or supporting ideas may lack focus.
bands.6: Addresses all parts of the task although some parts may be more fully covered than others; presents a relevant position although the conclusions may become unclear or repetitive; presents relevant main ideas but some may be inadequately developed/unclear.
bands.5: Addresses the task only partially; the format may be inappropriate in places; expresses a position but the development is not always clear and there may be no conclusion drawn; presents some main ideas but these are limited and not sufficiently developed; there may be irrelevant detail.
bands.4: Responds to the task only in a minimal way or the answer is tangential; the format may be inappropriate; presents a position but this is unclear; presents some main ideas but these are difficult to identify and may be repetitive, irrelevant or not well supported.
bands.3: Does not adequately address any part of the task; does not express a clear position; presents few ideas, which are largely undeveloped or irrelevant.
bands.2: Barely responds to the task; does not show a position; may attempt to present one or two ideas but there is no development.
bands.1: Answers are completely unrelated to the task; does not show any understanding of the task.
bands.0: Does not attend; does not attempt the task in any way; writes a wholly memorised response.

dimensions.name: coherence_cohesion
dimensions.description: Logical organisation; progression; cohesive devices; paragraphing.

bands.9: Uses cohesion in such a way that it attracts no attention; skilfully manages paragraphing.
bands.8: Sequences information and ideas logically; manages all aspects of cohesion well; uses paragraphing sufficiently and appropriately.
bands.7: Logically organises information and ideas; there is clear progression throughout; uses a range of cohesive devices appropriately although there may be some under-/over-use; presents a clear central topic within each paragraph.
bands.6: Arranges information and ideas coherently and there is a clear overall progression; uses cohesive devices effectively, but cohesion within and/or between sentences may be faulty or mechanical; may not always use referencing clearly or appropriately; uses paragraphing, but not always logically.
bands.5: Presents information with some organisation but there may be a lack of overall progression; makes inadequate, inaccurate or over-use of cohesive devices; may be repetitive because of lack of referencing and substitution; may not write in paragraphs, or paragraphing may be inadequate.
bands.4: Presents information and ideas but these are not arranged coherently and there is no clear progression in the response; uses some basic cohesive devices but these may be inaccurate or repetitive; may not write in paragraphs or uses them inadequately.
bands.3: Does not organise ideas logically; may use a very limited range of cohesive devices, and those used may not indicate a logical relationship between ideas.
bands.2: Has very little control of organisational features.
bands.0: Does not attend; does not attempt the task in any way; writes a wholly memorised response.

dimensions.name: lexical_resource
dimensions.description: Range and accuracy of vocabulary; spelling and word formation.

bands.9: Uses a wide range of vocabulary with very natural and sophisticated control of lexical features; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
bands.8: Uses a wide range of vocabulary fluently and flexibly to convey precise meanings; skilfully uses uncommon lexical items but there may be occasional inaccuracies in word choice and collocation; produces rare errors in spelling and/or word formation.
bands.7: Uses a sufficient range of vocabulary to allow some flexibility and precision; uses less common lexical items with some awareness of style and collocation; may produce occasional errors in word choice, spelling and/or word formation.
bands.6: Uses an adequate range of vocabulary for the task; attempts to use less common vocabulary but with some inaccuracy; makes some errors in spelling and/or word formation, but they do not impede communication.
bands.5: Uses a limited range of vocabulary, but this is minimally adequate for the task; may make noticeable errors in spelling and/or word formation that may cause some difficulty for the reader.
bands.4: Uses only basic vocabulary which may be used repetitively or which may be inappropriate for the task; has limited control of word formation and/or spelling; errors may cause strain for the reader.
bands.3: Uses only a very limited range of words and expressions with very limited control of word formation and/or spelling; errors may severely distort the message.
bands.2: Uses an extremely limited range of vocabulary; essentially no control of word formation and/or spelling.
bands.1: Can only use a few isolated words.
bands.0: Does not attend; does not attempt the task in any way; writes a wholly memorised response.

dimensions.name: grammatical_range_accuracy
dimensions.description: Range and accuracy of grammatical structures; punctuation.

bands.9: Uses a wide range of structures with full flexibility and accuracy; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
bands.8: Uses a wide range of structures flexibly; majority of sentences are error-free; makes only very occasional errors or inappropriacies.
bands.7: Uses a variety of complex structures; produces frequent error-free sentences; has good control of grammar and punctuation but may make a few errors.
bands.6: Uses a mix of simple and complex sentence forms; makes some errors in grammar and punctuation but they rarely reduce communication.
bands.5: Uses only a limited range of structures; attempts complex sentences but these tend to be less accurate than simple sentences; may make frequent grammatical errors and punctuation may be faulty; errors can cause some difficulty for the reader.
bands.4: Uses only a very limited range of structures with only rare use of subordinate clauses; some structures are accurate but errors predominate, and punctuation is often faulty; errors can cause some difficulty for the reader.
bands.3: Uses sentence forms but errors predominate and punctuation is often faulty; errors may severely distort the message.
bands.2: Cannot use sentence forms except in memorised phrases.
bands.1: Cannot use sentence forms at all.
bands.0: Does not attend; does not attempt the task in any way; writes a wholly memorised response.

hard_rules: Minimum 250 words for Task 2; underlength may reduce Task Response.
Address all parts of the task; maintain a clear position when the task requires it.
Wholly memorised responses may receive Band 0 for Task Response.
Off-topic or tangential answers are scored according to the degree they address the task.
Incomplete responses (e.g. missing a required part of a two-part question) affect Task Response.
